steps.title: Detection Log Tour
steps.text: This tour walks through the Detection Details page — the real-time log of every bird detection recorded by BCMS. It's auto-populated by the system; no manual input is needed.

steps.title: Top Bar Controls
steps.text:
Calendar icon (top-right): select the date range for displayed entries.
Printer / Download icon (top-left): export the log as XLSX, ODF, or PDF.
Two-arrow icon: reset all table filters to default.
Grid icon: choose which columns to show.

steps.title: Key Log Columns
steps.text:
Each row is one detection event. Important columns include:
• Local Risk Index — color-coded risk (blue → red)
• Species — auto-classified by AI; unidentified if too far or not in database
• Sector — where the event occurred
• Event — standing or flying
• Number / Speed / Height — quantity and motion data

steps.title: Row Actions
steps.text:
At the end of each row you'll find two icons:
• Camera icon — opens the Video Viewer to watch the detection recording
• Edit icon (blue) — manually correct the event (e.g. wrong species label, expert review)

Double-clicking any row opens a full JSON data pop-up that can be copied or downloaded.

steps.title: Filtering & Sorting
steps.text: The first row of the table acts as a live filter. Type in any cell to narrow results — filter by camera, species, sector, or risk level simultaneously. Click any column header to sort ascending or descending.

steps.title: Detection Tour Complete ✓
steps.text: You've covered the full Detection log. Next, explore Impact to learn how bird-strike incidents are manually reported, or check Statistics → Detection for trend charts.
